CREATE TABLE test_rand (id INT, seed INT);
SELECT id, rand(seed) FROM test_rand WHERE seed < 10;
WITH rand_values AS (SELECT id, rand(seed) AS random_value FROM test_rand WHERE seed >= 0) SELECT * FROM rand_values WHERE random_value > 1;
CREATE TABLE test_rand2 (seed INT PRIMARY KEY, random_number INT);
INSERT INTO test_rand2 SELECT seed, rand(seed) FROM test_rand;
SELECT t1.seed, t2.random_number FROM test_rand t1 JOIN test_rand2 t2 ON t1.seed = t2.seed WHERE t1.id = 1;
SELECT seed, COUNT(*) FROM test_rand GROUP BY seed HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT DISTINCT rand(seed) FROM test_rand WHERE seed IN (SELECT seed FROM test_rand2 WHERE random_number > 0);
